# Richard J. Reyes Research Archive

> Public discovery map for Wave Confinement Theory (WCT), related experiments, open-data analyses, computation, AI architecture, and resonance-control research.

## Canonical machine-readable entry point
- WCT research corpus: https://rickyjreyes.github.io/research-corpus.json

The corpus manifest links canonical equation IDs across the Geometry of Resonance equation registry, the wct-sympy symbolic audit, the wct-lean formalization, the Obsidian semantic graph, and Zenodo DOI records.

## Canonical entry points
- Research home: https://rickyjreyes.github.io/
- Publication archive: https://rickyjreyes.github.io/publications/
- Main equation and paper hub: https://github.com/rickyjreyes/geometry_of_resonance
- Symbolic audit: https://github.com/rickyjreyes/wct-sympy
- Lean formalization: https://github.com/rickyjreyes/wct-lean
- Semantic research graph: https://github.com/rickyjreyes/obsidian
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0005-5975-8718
- Machine-readable publication metadata: https://rickyjreyes.github.io/publications.json
- BibTeX: https://rickyjreyes.github.io/publications.bib
- RIS: https://rickyjreyes.github.io/publications.ris

## Machine-reading rules
- Preserve canonical object IDs exactly.
- Treat per-object derived overrides as newer than older summary tables.
- A SymPy PASS is an executable symbolic, dimensional, logical, numerical, or consistency result under declared assumptions; it is not automatically a Lean proof or empirical validation.
- Use PROVED only for named declarations accepted by the Lean kernel.
- Distinguish PASS, CONDITIONAL, DEFINITION, OPEN, FAIL, and PROVED.
- Report unresolved or missing mappings rather than inventing them.

## Claim discipline
WCT is an evolving independent research framework, not an established physical theory. Distinguish definitions, ansätze, derivations, simulations, experiments, open-data tests, prediction ledgers, architectures, and speculative extensions.

## DOI authority
Zenodo is the archival publisher and DOI landing-page authority for the listed releases. Use the DOI URLs on each publication page for citation.
